task_completion.description: Open the manuscript and style guide
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) open with 'crispr_brassica_manuscript.odt' and a text editor open with 'journal_style_guide.txt'.
The title 'High-efficiency CRISPR-Cas9 multiplex gene editing in Brassica napus' is visually distinct from the body text (e.g., larger font, bold, centered) according to standard title formatting.

task_completion.description: Apply heading hierarchies for H1 and H2 sections
task_completion.visual_evidence: H1 sections (e.g., 'Abstract', 'Introduction', 'Materials and Methods') and H2 sections (e.g., 'Plant material and growth conditions') are formatted with distinct heading styles, visually differentiating them from regular body text and each other.
Body paragraphs (e.g., starting with 'Targeted genome editing...') are consistently aligned (e.g., justified or left-aligned) as opposed to raw unformatted text.

The paragraph under 'Abstract' (containing 'Brassica napus (rapeseed) is an important oilseed crop') is block-indented, meaning its left and/or right margins are visibly brought in compared to the rest of the body text.

task_completion.description: Apply hanging indents to the bibliography
task_completion.visual_evidence: The paragraphs under the 'References' section are formatted with a hanging indent (the first line of each reference is flush left, and subsequent lines are indented).
The word processor shows no 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename), or a save action/dialog is visible in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI word processor to format the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice Writer or a similar word processor, rather than using terminal commands or python scripts to modify the ODT file directly.

integrity.description: Agent used proper formatting tools rather than manual spacing
integrity.visual_evidence: Formatting (like hanging indents or block indents) is achieved using the ruler, paragraph settings, or styles pane, rather than manually typing spaces or tabs at the start of lines.
The specific text for the title, authors, abstract, and headings remains exactly as provided in the privileged information, without being overwritten or deleted.
